exemplar.title: Which Argentinian economic policies between 2020 and 2024 contribute to high inflation, and how did these impact economic stability?

detailedExemplarData.title: Which Argentinian economic policies between 2020 and 2024 contribute to high inflation, and how did these impact economic stability?

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The topic and period are clearly identified, giving a coherent investigative framework.
Research question is explicitly stated with defined time frame and dual focus.
The methodology references relevant secondary sources and comparative approach.
weaknesses: Scope remains too broad, blurring a clear causal focus.
Research question is compound and lacks operational precision.
Methodology lacks detail on analytical tools, selection criteria and data processing.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The strand assessments situate the work firmly in the middle of the 3â4 band for topic communication, but in the lower 1â2 band for research question and methodology. Averaging these performances yields a midâband performance worthy of 3 out of 6. This reflects a submission that communicates its purpose adequately but does not achieve the clear, tightly focused design or methodological rigor of the top band.

strengths: Economic terminology is used accurately and consistently throughout.
The student demonstrates a general understanding of inflation theory and relevant macroeconomic concepts.
A broad range of source material is cited, indicating familiarity with diverse perspectives.
weaknesses: Source application is often descriptive rather than driving analysis.
Reliability and context of secondary data are not critically interrogated.
Knowledge explanations sometimes conflate short-run and long-run effects without supporting evidence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With two strands assessed in the 1â2 band (application of sources and topic knowledge) and one in the 3â4 band (terminology), the overall performance sits at the lower end of the 3â4 band. Awarding 3 out of 6 acknowledges the clarity and coherence of subject-specific language, balanced against the descriptive application of material and uneven critical engagement with sources.

strengths: A range of relevant secondary research is gathered, demonstrating some alignment with the research question.
Analytical frameworks (AD/AS, exchange-rate models) are applied consistently to the material presented.
The essay constructs a coherent narrative linking policy eras to inflation outcomes.
weaknesses: Research selection is driven more by availability than a strategic investigative plan.
Analysis is often illustrative rather than data-driven, with causal links asserted rather than demonstrated.
Evaluation remains largely non-critical and descriptive, with limited counter-evidence or methodological reflection.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the work draws on pertinent research and employs economic models, the depth of analysis and critical evaluation remains at the top of the 1â3 band. A score of 4/12 recognizes that the student has moved beyond purely descriptive treatment but falls short of the sustained, well-supported analysis and critical evaluation required for higher bands.

strengths: The essay follows the required structural conventions (contents page, title page, section headings).
Basic formattingâfont consistency and page numberingâis correctly applied.
weaknesses: Key elements (abstract, consistent sub-section numbering, signposting) are missing or uneven.
Figures lack consistent numbering and captions; citation style is irregular.
Transitions between sections are abrupt, hindering the flow of argument.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets the descriptors for the 1â2 band: generally appropriate structure with several missing formal elements, and only partial formatting consistency. A middleâband score of 2 out of 4 fairly reflects this level of presentation quality.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is stated explicitly and contains a time frame (2020-2024) and a dual focus on policies and domestic stability; however, **it remains broad and somewhat compound**, making it difficult to treat in the depth expected for an EE. The question does not narrow the investigation to a clearly operationalisable set of variables (e.g., a single policy instrument or market). As a result, the ensuing discussion often drifts between monetary, fiscal and exchange-rate measures without a tight analytical thread. This places it in the 1-2 band where the RQ is present but insufficiently focused and only loosely connected to later discussion.

The title and opening paragraphs clearly identify Argentina, the 2020-24 period and the broad theme of inflation, so the reader can understand what is being investigated. Nevertheless, the scope is **very wide (several policy areas, two different presidencies and the whole economy)** and the wording shifts between âcontribute to high inflationâ and âimpact economic stability,â blurring the central purpose. Because the student tries to analyse multiple policies at the macro level rather than one focused causal link that can be systematically investigated, the focus is only partially appropriate. Hence the work meets the descriptors for the 3-4 band but does not achieve the precision or tight focus required for the 5-6 band.

The bibliography is extensive and the essay quotes many figures, yet **the material is often inserted to illustrate rather than to drive analysis**. Sources such as blogs, videos and newspaper reports are treated at face value, and statistical data are quoted without critical interrogation of reliability or context. Consequently, relevance is uneven and the application remains mostly descriptive, consistent with the 1-2 band.

The essay follows the required EE sections and includes a contents page, but some formal elements (e.g., abstract, consistent sub-section numbering, effective signposting) are missing or uneven. Transitions between major sections are abrupt, and the breadth of the topic makes the overall line of argument hard to follow. These issues indicate a structure that is âgenerally appropriate with some missing elements,â placing it in the 1-2 band.

Basic formatting conventions are observed: a title page, page numbers, and consistent font. However, figures lack numbering consistency, some captions are duplicated, and citation style is irregular (mix of footnotes and inline links). Minor typographical errors and occasional broken links detract from professional presentation. Thus, layout and formatting meet expectations only partially, corresponding to the 1-2 band.

While the essay attempts to weigh the impacts of different policy eras, the evaluation remains **largely superficial**âfor example, benefits and drawbacks of price controls or âshock therapyâ are described but not balanced with counter-evidence or alternative perspectives. The argument lacks a sustained, logical structure and the conclusion largely restates earlier points. This aligns with the mid-range of the 1-3 band, where evaluation is present but non-critical and weakly reasoned.

The student provides standard graphical analysis (AD/AS, exchange-rate S-D, PPC), but these are used illustratively rather than as platforms for rigorous, data-driven analysis. **Causal links are asserted more than demonstrated**, and numerical evidence is seldom integrated into the diagrams. Conclusions on policy effectiveness rely on secondary opinions rather than the studentâs own quantitative findings. This represents partial, sometimes unsupported analysis, fitting the top of the 1-3 band.

The student demonstrates a general understanding of inflation, AD/AS and exchange-rate theory, but explanations sometimes conflate short-run and long-run effects or attribute large macro outcomes to single diagrams without supporting evidence. **Knowledge is largely narrative and anecdotal**, relying heavily on secondary commentary rather than disciplined economic reasoning, which fits the 1-2 band where knowledge is clear at times but essentially descriptive.

The research draws on a wide range of secondary material, but **selection is driven more by availability than by a coherent investigative plan**. Some key macro data (e.g., money supply growth, inflation expectations indices) are mentioned but not systematically analysed, and there is minimal triangulation or primary data collection. The majority of sources are nonetheless pertinent to the topic, situating the strand in the upper end of the 1-3 band.

The methodology section lists secondary sources (IMF, BCRA, news outlets) and notes an intention to compare the FernÃ¡ndez and Milei administrations, but **it stops at description**: there is no clear explanation of how data will be selected, what analytical tools (e.g., econometric tests, specific indicators) will be applied, nor any acknowledgment of limitations beyond a brief mention of media bias. The range of methods is therefore limited and evidence of informed, purposeful selection is weak, aligning with the 1-2 band.

Economic terminology (e.g., price ceilings, fiscal dominance, quantity theory of money) is generally accurate and used consistently throughout the essay. Occasional slips (âpickâ for âpeakâ, confusion between âpesoâ plural and singular) occur but do not impede meaning. The overall use of concepts therefore reaches the 3-4 band, albeit at the lower end because definitions are sometimes superficial or unsupported by data.

annotations.comment: The research question is compound and overly broad, encompassing multiple policy types and impacts. For a focused investigation, specify a single policy instrument or economic indicator to operationalize the question and ensure a clear analytical thread.
The word count is not provided at the start of the report, which is a formatting requirement. Please include the word count at the beginning according to IB guidelines.
An abstract is missing. Including a concise abstract summarising the research question, methodology and key findings would improve structure and signposting for the reader.

The discussion of the Consumer Price Index is informative but does not link directly back to economic stability or the research question. Strengthen relevance by connecting CPI trends to policy impacts on market stability.

The title addresses a wide range of policies and outcomes, diluting the scope. Narrow the title to a specific policy or mechanism to improve focus and align with the systematic investigation required.
There is no section reflecting on research decision-making, limitations or planning. Adding a reflective commentary would demonstrate critical engagement and meet IB reflection expectations.
Figure numbering and captions are inconsistent (e.g. âFigure.1â appears twice). Adopt a uniform numbering and caption style and ensure cross-references match throughout the essay.
Caption style shifts again here (âFigure.2â vs earlier format). Maintain consistent formatting for figure labels to enhance professional presentation.
The SRAS shift in Figure 2 is asserted without explaining its causal link to policy changes. Clarify why supply conditions would improve or worsen under the specific shock therapy measures.
Figure 3 projects government debt to GDP through 2029 but omits the forecasting methodology. Outline assumptions and data sources underlying these projections to validate their use in your analysis.

The quantity theory of money is cited (MV=PT) but not applied with Argentine money supply and price level data. Incorporate empirical M, V, P, and T values to demonstrate the theoryâs explanatory power.

The price ceiling graph is correctly drawn but remains illustrative. Integrate Argentine-specific data (e.g. actual ceiling levels, shortage volumes) to ground the model in empirical evidence and deepen analysis.

The conclusion summarises findings but lacks critical evaluation of methodological limitations. Reflect on data constraints and potential biases to strengthen the evaluative component of your argument.
The bibliography demonstrates breadth but displays inconsistent citation formatting. Adopt a single style (e.g. APA or Chicago) and apply it uniformly across all entries.
Footnotes mix inline URLs and full references inconsistently. Standardise footnote structure (author, date, title, publisher) to improve readability and professional presentation.
The definition of hyperinflation (>50% per month) is conflated with annual rates later. Clarify whether the discussion refers to monthly or annual inflation to maintain conceptual accuracy.

The methodology outlines source types but lacks detail on analytical tools, selection criteria and data processing steps. Specify which econometric or statistical methods will be used and how data will be sampled.

The essay lists sources from Statista and Trading Economics without assessing their limitations or reliability. Discuss source validity and potential biases to demonstrate critical evaluation of reference material.
The contextual description is comprehensive but largely narrative. To strengthen relevance, embed specific data points or analyse how each political transition directly influenced inflation drivers.

Exchange rate analysis describes shifts but lacks quantitative measures of volatility. Include exchange rate variance or standard deviation to substantiate claims about instabilityâs inflationary impact.

The statement on Argentinaâs inelastic import dependence would benefit from citation of import elasticity data. Provide empirical evidence to support the claim and strengthen the argumentâs credibility.

Appendix 1 presents monthly inflation data but is never referenced or analysed in the main text. Integrate these figures into the analysis or refer explicitly to the appendix to justify its inclusion.
